### Primary and secondary education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Primary and secondary education")]

Ottawa has four main public school boards: English, English-Catholic, French, and French-Catholic. The English-language [Ottawa-Carleton District School Board](/wiki/Ottawa-Carleton_District_School_Board "Ottawa-Carleton District School Board") (OCDSB) is the largest board with 116 primary schools, 26 scondary schools, 5 alternative learning sites and 1 adult education school,[[260]](#cite_note-261) followed by the English-Catholic [Ottawa Catholic School Board](/wiki/Ottawa_Catholic_School_Board "Ottawa Catholic School Board") with 90 schools.[[261]](#cite_note-262) The two French-language boards are the French-Catholic *[Conseil des écoles catholiques du Centre-Est](/wiki/Conseil_des_%C3%A9coles_catholiques_du_Centre-Est "Conseil des écoles catholiques du Centre-Est")* with 46 primary schools and 11 secondary schools,[[262]](#cite_note-263) and the French *[Conseil des écoles publiques de l'Est de l'Ontario](/wiki/Conseil_des_%C3%A9coles_publiques_de_l%27Est_de_l%27Ontario "Conseil des écoles publiques de l'Est de l'Ontario")* with 44 schools.[[263]](#cite_note-264) Ottawa also has numerous [private schools](/wiki/Private_school "Private school") which are not part of a board.[[264]](#cite_note-265)

The [Ottawa Public Library](/wiki/Ottawa_Public_Library "Ottawa Public Library") was created in 1906 as part of the [Carnegie library](/wiki/Carnegie_library "Carnegie library") system.[[265]](#cite_note-266) As of 2026 the library system has 1.8 million items, down from 2.3 million in 34 branches and 2 mobile libraries in 2008.[[266]](#cite_note-267)[[267]](#cite_note-268) Approximately 11.5 million loans were conducted in 2024,[[268]](#cite_note-:15-269) approximately 8.2 million physical loans and the remainder digital items.[[269]](#cite_note-270) In 2024, the library saw 3.5 million visits.[[268]](#cite_note-:15-269)

### Higher education and public research

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Higher education and public research")]

Ottawa is known as the most educated city in Canada, with over half the population having graduated from college and/or university.[[270]](#cite_note-271) As of 2007, Ottawa was claimed by the local business journal to have the highest per capita concentration of [engineers](/wiki/Engineer "Engineer"), [scientists](/wiki/Scientist "Scientist"), and residents with [PhDs](/wiki/PhD "PhD") in Canada.[[271]](#cite_note-272) The city has two main public universities and two main public colleges.

* [Carleton University](/wiki/Carleton_University "Carleton University") was founded in 1942 to meet the needs of returning World War II veterans and later became Ontario's first private, non-denominational college. Over time, Carleton transitioned into a comprehensive university that Maclean's ranks 4th out of 15 in its comprehensive category.[[272]](#cite_note-273) The university's campus sits between [Old Ottawa South](/wiki/Old_Ottawa_South "Old Ottawa South") and the [Central Experimental Farm](/wiki/Central_Experimental_Farm "Central Experimental Farm").[[273]](#cite_note-274)
* The [University of Ottawa](/wiki/University_of_Ottawa "University of Ottawa") (originally named the "College of Bytown") was the first post-secondary institution established in the city in 1848. The university later grew to become the largest English-French bilingual university in the world.[[274]](#cite_note-Quick-275) It is also a member of the [U15](/wiki/U15_Group_of_Canadian_Research_Universities "U15 Group of Canadian Research Universities"), a group of highly respected research-intensive universities in Canada.[[275]](#cite_note-276) The university's main campus is in the [Sandy Hill](/wiki/Sandy_Hill,_Ottawa "Sandy Hill, Ottawa") neighbourhood, just adjacent to the city's downtown core.[[276]](#cite_note-277) The University of Ottawa's catholic [affiliated](/wiki/Affiliated_school "Affiliated school") university college is [St. Paul University](/wiki/Saint_Paul_University "Saint Paul University").
* [Algonquin College](/wiki/Algonquin_College "Algonquin College") is a college of applied arts and technology founded in 1967.[[277]](#cite_note-278) Its main campus is located in the [City View](/wiki/City_View,_Ottawa "City View, Ottawa") neighbourhood of [College Ward](/wiki/College_Ward "College Ward"). The college serves the [National Capital Region](/wiki/National_Capital_Region_(Canada) "National Capital Region (Canada)") and the outlying areas of [Eastern Ontario](/wiki/Eastern_Ontario "Eastern Ontario").[[278]](#cite_note-279) The college has satellite campuses in [Pembroke](/wiki/Pembroke,_Ontario "Pembroke, Ontario") and [Perth](/wiki/Perth,_Ontario "Perth, Ontario").[[279]](#cite_note-280)
* [Collège La Cité](/wiki/Coll%C3%A8ge_La_Cit%C3%A9 "Collège La Cité") is the largest French-language college in Ontario.[[280]](#cite_note-281) Founded in 1989,[[281]](#cite_note-282) its campus is located off the [Aviation Parkway](/wiki/Aviation_Parkway_(Ottawa) "Aviation Parkway (Ottawa)") in the [Carson Meadows](/wiki/Carson_Meadows "Carson Meadows") neighbourhood.[[282]](#cite_note-283)

Other colleges and universities in the metropolitan area are located in the neighbouring suburb of Gatineau, including the [University of Quebec in Outaouais](/wiki/Universit%C3%A9_du_Qu%C3%A9bec_en_Outaouais "Université du Québec en Outaouais"), [Cégep de l'Outaouais](/wiki/C%C3%A9gep_de_l%27Outaouais "Cégep de l'Outaouais"), and [Heritage College](/wiki/Heritage_College_(Gatineau) "Heritage College (Gatineau)").[[283]](#cite_note-284)

The city is also home to many federal post-secondary associations, including those representing institutions like [Polytechnics Canada](/wiki/Polytechnics_Canada "Polytechnics Canada"),[[284]](#cite_note-285) [Universities Canada](/wiki/Universities_Canada "Universities Canada"),[[285]](#cite_note-286) [Colleges and Institutes Canada](/wiki/Colleges_and_Institutes_Canada "Colleges and Institutes Canada"),[[286]](#cite_note-287) [Association des collèges et universités de la francophonie canadienne](/wiki/Association_des_coll%C3%A8ges_et_universit%C3%A9s_de_la_francophonie_canadienne "Association des collèges et universités de la francophonie canadienne"),[[287]](#cite_note-288) and the U15;[[288]](#cite_note-289) those representing educators, such as the [Canadian Association of University Teachers](/wiki/Canadian_Association_of_University_Teachers "Canadian Association of University Teachers");[[289]](#cite_note-290) and those representing students, such as the [Canadian Alliance of Student Associations](/wiki/Canadian_Alliance_of_Student_Associations "Canadian Alliance of Student Associations"),[[290]](#cite_note-291) [Canadian Federation of Students](/wiki/Canadian_Federation_of_Students "Canadian Federation of Students")[[291]](#cite_note-292) and the National Educational Association of Disabled Students.[[292]](#cite_note-293)

Public research also occurs through government laboratories and research centres such as the [National Research Council](/wiki/National_Research_Council_Canada "National Research Council Canada"),[[293]](#cite_note-294) various [Health Canada](/wiki/Health_Canada "Health Canada") laboratories[[294]](#cite_note-295) or the [Central Experimental Farm](/wiki/Central_Experimental_Farm "Central Experimental Farm").[[295]](#cite_note-296)

### Public health

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Public health")]

See also: [List of hospitals in Ottawa](/wiki/List_of_hospitals_in_Ottawa "List of hospitals in Ottawa")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2f/Civic_Hospital%2C_Ottawa.JPG/250px-Civic_Hospital%2C_Ottawa.JPG)](/wiki/File:Civic_Hospital,_Ottawa.JPG)

The [Civic Hospital](/wiki/Ottawa_Civic_Hospital "Ottawa Civic Hospital") is one of three main campuses of [The Ottawa Hospital](/wiki/The_Ottawa_Hospital "The Ottawa Hospital").

There are six active general medical hospitals in the city of Ottawa: The [Queensway Carleton Hospital](/wiki/Queensway_Carleton_Hospital "Queensway Carleton Hospital"), [The Ottawa Hospital](/wiki/The_Ottawa_Hospital "The Ottawa Hospital") ([Civic Hospital](/wiki/Ottawa_Civic_Hospital "Ottawa Civic Hospital"), [General Hospital](/wiki/Ottawa_General_Hospital "Ottawa General Hospital"), [Riverside Hospital](/wiki/Riverside_Hospital_of_Ottawa "Riverside Hospital of Ottawa")), [Montfort Hospital](/wiki/Montfort_Hospital "Montfort Hospital"), and [Children's Hospital of Eastern Ontario](/wiki/Children%27s_Hospital_of_Eastern_Ontario "Children's Hospital of Eastern Ontario"). Several specialized hospital facilities are also present, such as the [University of Ottawa Heart Institute](/wiki/University_of_Ottawa_Heart_Institute "University of Ottawa Heart Institute"), the [Royal Ottawa Mental Health Centre](/wiki/Royal_Ottawa_Mental_Health_Centre "Royal Ottawa Mental Health Centre"), and Élisabeth Bruyère Hospital.[[296]](#cite_note-297) There are also several hospitals and major medical centres in neighbouring suburban communities and commuter towns.[[297]](#cite_note-298) The [University of Ottawa Faculty of Medicine](/wiki/University_of_Ottawa_Faculty_of_Medicine "University of Ottawa Faculty of Medicine") operates [teaching hospitals](/wiki/Teaching_hospital "Teaching hospital") in conjunction with partners throughout the city.[[298]](#cite_note-uOttawa_Med-299)

Ottawa is the headquarters of numerous major medical organizations and institutions such as [Canadian Red Cross](/wiki/Canadian_Red_Cross "Canadian Red Cross"), [Canadian Blood Services](/wiki/Canadian_Blood_Services "Canadian Blood Services"), [Health Canada](/wiki/Health_Canada "Health Canada"), [Canadian Medical Association](/wiki/Canadian_Medical_Association "Canadian Medical Association"), [Royal College of Physicians and Surgeons of Canada](/wiki/Royal_College_of_Physicians_and_Surgeons_of_Canada "Royal College of Physicians and Surgeons of Canada"), [Canadian Nurses Association](/wiki/Canadian_Nurses_Association "Canadian Nurses Association"), and the [Medical Council of Canada](/wiki/Medical_Council_of_Canada "Medical Council of Canada").

## Culture and contemporary life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Culture and contemporary life")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cd/Byward_Market_Sunset.jpg/250px-Byward_Market_Sunset.jpg)](/wiki/File:Byward_Market_Sunset.jpg)

[ByWard Market](/wiki/ByWard_Market "ByWard Market") is a retail and entertainment district in Downtown Ottawa.

Traditionally, the [ByWard Market](/wiki/ByWard_Market "ByWard Market") (in Lower Town), Parliament Hill, [Chinatown](/wiki/Chinatown,_Ottawa "Chinatown, Ottawa"), and the [Golden Triangle](/wiki/Golden_Triangle_(Ottawa,_Canada) "Golden Triangle (Ottawa, Canada)") (in Centretown – Downtown) have been focal points of the cultural scenes in Ottawa.[[299]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEHale201159–60-300) Modern thoroughfares such as [Wellington Street](/wiki/Wellington_Street_(Ottawa) "Wellington Street (Ottawa)"), [Rideau Street](/wiki/Rideau_Street "Rideau Street"), [Sussex Drive](/wiki/Sussex_Drive "Sussex Drive"), [Elgin Street](/wiki/Elgin_Street_(Ottawa) "Elgin Street (Ottawa)"), [Bank Street](/wiki/Bank_Street_(Ottawa) "Bank Street (Ottawa)"), [Somerset Street](/wiki/Somerset_Street_(Ottawa) "Somerset Street (Ottawa)"), [Preston Street](/wiki/Preston_Street_(Ottawa) "Preston Street (Ottawa)"), Richmond Road in [Westboro](/wiki/Westboro,_Ottawa "Westboro, Ottawa"), and [Sparks Street](/wiki/Sparks_Street "Sparks Street") are home to many boutiques, museums, theatres, galleries, landmarks and memorials in addition to eating establishments, cafes, bars and nightclubs.[[300]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEHale201161–68-301)

As Canada's capital, Ottawa has played host to many significant cultural events in [Canadian history](/wiki/Canadian_history "Canadian history"), including the first visit of the reigning [Canadian sovereign](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Canada "Monarchy of Canada")—[King George VI](/wiki/George_VI "George VI"), with [his consort](/wiki/List_of_royal_consorts_of_Canada "List of royal consorts of Canada"), [Queen Elizabeth](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_The_Queen_Mother "Queen Elizabeth The Queen Mother")—[to his parliament, on 19 May 1939](/wiki/1939_royal_tour_of_Canada "1939 royal tour of Canada").[[301]](#cite_note-BousfieldToffoli1989-302) [VE Day](/wiki/Victory_in_Europe_Day "Victory in Europe Day") was marked with a large celebration on 8 May 1945,[[302]](#cite_note-Granatstein2005n-303) the first raising of [the country's new national flag](/wiki/Flag_of_Canada "Flag of Canada") took place on 15 February 1965,[[303]](#cite_note-Solski2006-304)[[304]](#cite_note-305) and the [centennial of Confederation](/wiki/Canadian_Centennial "Canadian Centennial") was celebrated on 1 July 1967.[[305]](#cite_note-306) Queen [Elizabeth II](/wiki/Elizabeth_II "Elizabeth II") was in Ottawa on 17 April 1982, to issue a [royal proclamation](/wiki/Proclamation "Proclamation") of the enactment of the [Constitution Act](/wiki/Constitution_Act,_1982 "Constitution Act, 1982").[[306]](#cite_note-Hayes2008-307) In 1983, then prince [Charles](/wiki/Prince_Charles "Prince Charles") and [Diana Princess of Wales](/wiki/Diana_Princess_of_Wales "Diana Princess of Wales") came to Ottawa for a state dinner hosted by then Prime Minister [Pierre Trudeau](/wiki/Pierre_Trudeau "Pierre Trudeau").[[307]](#cite_note-308) In 2011, Ottawa was selected as the first city to receive [Prince William, Duke of Cambridge](/wiki/Prince_William,_Duke_of_Cambridge "Prince William, Duke of Cambridge"), and [Catherine, Duchess of Cambridge](/wiki/Catherine,_Duchess_of_Cambridge "Catherine, Duchess of Cambridge") during their [tour of Canada](/wiki/2011_royal_tour_of_Canada "2011 royal tour of Canada").[[308]](#cite_note-2011-06-30-HCH-309)

Ottawa was featured in the short story collection *[For Your Eyes Only](/wiki/For_Your_Eyes_Only_(short_story_collection) "For Your Eyes Only (short story collection)")*, by [Ian Fleming](/wiki/Ian_Fleming "Ian Fleming").[[309]](#cite_note-310)

[![The National War Memorial](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4c/War_Memorial_Guards_Ottawa.jpg/250px-War_Memorial_Guards_Ottawa.jpg)](/wiki/File:War_Memorial_Guards_Ottawa.jpg)

The [National War Memorial](/wiki/National_War_Memorial_(Canada) "National War Memorial (Canada)")

### Landmarks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Landmarks")]

Main articles: [List of National Historic Sites of Canada in Ottawa](/wiki/List_of_National_Historic_Sites_of_Canada_in_Ottawa "List of National Historic Sites of Canada in Ottawa") and [List of designated heritage properties in Ottawa](/wiki/List_of_designated_heritage_properties_in_Ottawa "List of designated heritage properties in Ottawa")

There is one [World Heritage Site](/wiki/World_Heritage_Site "World Heritage Site") in Ottawa, the [Rideau Canal](/wiki/Rideau_Canal "Rideau Canal"),[[310]](#cite_note-311) along with 27 [National Historic Sites of Canada](/wiki/National_Historic_Site_of_Canada "National Historic Site of Canada") in Ottawa, including the Château Laurier, the [Parliament of Canada](/wiki/Parliament_of_Canada "Parliament of Canada"), Confederation Square, the former Ottawa Teachers' College and [Laurier House](/wiki/Laurier_House "Laurier House").[[311]](#cite_note-312) Additionally in 1990, Notre Dame Cathedral Basilica was designated as a National Historic Site.[[312]](#cite_note-313) Many other properties of cultural value have been designated as having "heritage elements" by the City of Ottawa under Part IV of the *[Ontario Heritage Act](/wiki/Ontario_Heritage_Act "Ontario Heritage Act")*.[[313]](#cite_note-314)

### Arts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Arts")]

See also: [List of attractions in Ottawa](/wiki/List_of_attractions_in_Ottawa "List of attractions in Ottawa") and [List of museums in Ottawa](/wiki/List_of_museums_in_Ottawa "List of museums in Ottawa")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/06/CanadianMuseumofNature2010-05-19.JPG/250px-CanadianMuseumofNature2010-05-19.JPG)](/wiki/File:CanadianMuseumofNature2010-05-19.JPG)

The [Canadian Museum of Nature](/wiki/Canadian_Museum_of_Nature "Canadian Museum of Nature") is Canada's national museum of [natural history](/wiki/Natural_history "Natural history") and [natural science](/wiki/Natural_science "Natural science").

#### Performing and visual arts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=30 "Edit section: Performing and visual arts")]

The [Ottawa Little Theatre](/wiki/Ottawa_Little_Theatre "Ottawa Little Theatre"), founded in 1913 as the Ottawa Drama League, is Ottawa's longest-running community theatre company.[[314]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEHale201160-315) Since 1969, Ottawa has been the home of the National Arts Centre, a major performing arts venue that houses four stages and is home to the [National Arts Centre Orchestra](/wiki/National_Arts_Centre_Orchestra "National Arts Centre Orchestra"), and formerly hosted the [Opera Lyra Ottawa](/wiki/Opera_Lyra_Ottawa "Opera Lyra Ottawa") and the [Ottawa Symphony Orchestra](/wiki/Ottawa_Symphony_Orchestra "Ottawa Symphony Orchestra") prior to their failure and move to Carleton University, respectively.[[315]](#cite_note-316)[[316]](#cite_note-317)

Established in 1975, the [Great Canadian Theatre Company](/wiki/Great_Canadian_Theatre_Company "Great Canadian Theatre Company") specializes in the production of Canadian plays at a local level.[[317]](#cite_note-318) The cities museum landscape is notable for containing six of Canada's nine national museums, the [Canada Agriculture and Food Museum](/wiki/Canada_Agriculture_Museum "Canada Agriculture Museum"), the [Canada Aviation and Space Museum](/wiki/Canada_Aviation_and_Space_Museum "Canada Aviation and Space Museum"), the [Canada Science and Technology Museum](/wiki/Canada_Science_and_Technology_Museum "Canada Science and Technology Museum"), Canadian Museum of Nature, [Canadian War Museum](/wiki/Canadian_War_Museum "Canadian War Museum") and National Gallery of Canada.[[318]](#cite_note-319) The [National Gallery of Canada](/wiki/National_Gallery_of_Canada "National Gallery of Canada"); designed by famous architect [Moshe Safdie](/wiki/Moshe_Safdie "Moshe Safdie"), it is a permanent home to the [*Maman*](/wiki/Maman_(sculpture) "Maman (sculpture)") sculpture.[[319]](#cite_note-320) The [Canadian War Museum](/wiki/Canadian_War_Museum "Canadian War Museum") houses a collection comprised of approximately 600,000 artifacts, photos and books and was moved to an expanded facility in 2005.[[320]](#cite_note-321)[[321]](#cite_note-322) The Victoria Memorial Museum Building, which houses the [Canadian Museum of Nature](/wiki/Canadian_Museum_of_Nature "Canadian Museum of Nature") commenced construction in 1905 and it opened as the National Museum of Natural Sciences in 1912, and underwent a major renovation between 2004 and 2010, leading to a centrepiece Blue Whale skeleton, and the creation of a monthly nightclub experience, *Nature Nocturne*.[[322]](#cite_note-323)[[323]](#cite_note-324)[[324]](#cite_note-325)

### Cuisine

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=31 "Edit section: Cuisine")]

Ottawa is home to several regional dishes. As a city with traditional [French-Canadian](/wiki/French-Canadian_cuisine "French-Canadian cuisine") roots, staples such as [poutine](/wiki/Poutine "Poutine") are served throughout the city. However, many consider [shawarma](/wiki/Shawarma "Shawarma") Ottawa's official dish;[[325]](#cite_note-326)[[326]](#cite_note-Official_dish-327) the city contains more shawarma restaurants than anywhere else in Canada.[[327]](#cite_note-Shawarma_Capital-328) [Beaver tails](/wiki/Fried_dough "Fried dough"), a fried dough pastry, were first created in Ottawa in the 1970s. [Le Cordon Bleu](/wiki/Le_Cordon_Bleu "Le Cordon Bleu") has a long-established culinary arts institute in the central Ottawa neighbourhood of Sandy Hill, the only Le Cordon Bleu campus in North America.[[328]](#cite_note-Le_Cordon_Bleu-329)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9d/Ice_slide_Winterlude_Ottawa_2007.jpg/250px-Ice_slide_Winterlude_Ottawa_2007.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ice_slide_Winterlude_Ottawa_2007.jpg)

[Winterlude](/wiki/Winterlude "Winterlude") is an annual [winter festival](/wiki/Winter_festival "Winter festival") held in Ottawa.

### Festivals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Festivals")]

See also: [List of festivals in Ottawa](/wiki/List_of_festivals_in_Ottawa "List of festivals in Ottawa")

Ottawa hosts a variety of annual seasonal activities—such as [Winterlude](/wiki/Winterlude "Winterlude"), the largest festival in Canada,[[329]](#cite_note-330) and [Canada Day](/wiki/Canada_Day "Canada Day") celebrations on Parliament Hill and surrounding downtown area,[[330]](#cite_note-331) as well as [Bluesfest](/wiki/Ottawa_Bluesfest "Ottawa Bluesfest"), [Canadian Tulip Festival](/wiki/Canadian_Tulip_Festival "Canadian Tulip Festival"), [Ottawa Dragon Boat Festival](/wiki/Ottawa_Dragon_Boat_Festival "Ottawa Dragon Boat Festival"), [Ottawa International Jazz Festival](/wiki/Ottawa_International_Jazz_Festival "Ottawa International Jazz Festival"), [Fringe Festival](/wiki/Ottawa_Fringe_Festival "Ottawa Fringe Festival"), [Capital Pride](/wiki/Capital_Pride_(Ottawa) "Capital Pride (Ottawa)"), and [CityFolk](/wiki/CityFolk_Festival "CityFolk Festival"), that have grown to become some of the largest festivals of their kind in the world.[[331]](#cite_note-332)[[332]](#cite_note-333)[[333]](#cite_note-334)[[334]](#cite_note-335)[[335]](#cite_note-336)[[336]](#cite_note-337) In 2010, Ottawa's Festival industry received the [IFEA](/wiki/International_Festivals_and_Events_Association "International Festivals and Events Association") "World Festival and Event City Award" for the category of North American cities with a population between 500,000 and 1,000,000.[[337]](#cite_note-338)

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Sports")]

Further information: [Sport in Ottawa](/wiki/Sport_in_Ottawa "Sport in Ottawa") and [List of Ottawa parks](/wiki/List_of_Ottawa_parks "List of Ottawa parks")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8d/TD_Place_Canal.jpg/250px-TD_Place_Canal.jpg)](/wiki/File:TD_Place_Canal.jpg)

[TD Place Stadium](/wiki/TD_Place_Stadium "TD Place Stadium") is home to the [CFL](/wiki/Canadian_Football_League "Canadian Football League")'s [Ottawa Redblacks](/wiki/Ottawa_Redblacks "Ottawa Redblacks"), the [CPL](/wiki/Canadian_Premier_League "Canadian Premier League")'s [Atlético Ottawa](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Ottawa "Atlético Ottawa"), and the [NSL](/wiki/Northern_Super_League "Northern Super League")'s [Ottawa Rapid FC](/wiki/Ottawa_Rapid_FC "Ottawa Rapid FC").

### Professional sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=34 "Edit section: Professional sports")]

[Sport in Ottawa](/wiki/Sport_in_Ottawa "Sport in Ottawa") has a history dating back to the 19th century. The city is currently home to six professional sports teams. The [Ottawa Senators](/wiki/Ottawa_Senators "Ottawa Senators") are a professional ice hockey team playing in the [National Hockey League](/wiki/National_Hockey_League "National Hockey League").[[338]](#cite_note-339) The team shares the name with a [previous franchise](/wiki/Ottawa_Senators_(original) "Ottawa Senators (original)") that won the [Stanley Cup](/wiki/Stanley_Cup "Stanley Cup") four times between 1920 and 1927.[[339]](#cite_note-340)[[340]](#cite_note-341) The team is currently a member of the Atlantic Division and play their home games at the [Canadian Tire Centre](/wiki/Canadian_Tire_Centre "Canadian Tire Centre").[[341]](#cite_note-WeekesBanks2002-342) In 2023, the [Ottawa Charge](/wiki/Ottawa_Charge "Ottawa Charge") became one of the six charter franchises of the [Professional Women's Hockey League](/wiki/Professional_Women%27s_Hockey_League "Professional Women's Hockey League") (PWHL).The Charge play home games at [TD Place Arena](/wiki/TD_Place_Arena "TD Place Arena").[[342]](#cite_note-343)

The [Ottawa Redblacks](/wiki/Ottawa_Redblacks "Ottawa Redblacks") are a professional Canadian Football team playing in the [Canadian Football League](/wiki/Canadian_Football_League "Canadian Football League").[[343]](#cite_note-Ottawa_CFL-344) Formerly the [Ottawa Rough Riders](/wiki/Ottawa_Rough_Riders "Ottawa Rough Riders") represented the city until 1996. With a precursors dating back to 1876, the team adopted its identify in 1898 and was one of the oldest and longest-lived professional sports teams in North America.[[344]](#cite_note-345) The professional soccer club, [Atlético Ottawa](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Ottawa "Atlético Ottawa"), plays in the [Canadian Premier League](/wiki/Canadian_Premier_League "Canadian Premier League"). The team was founded by Spanish club [Atlético Madrid](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Madrid "Atlético Madrid"), and along with the Redblacks, play their home games at [TD Place Stadium](/wiki/TD_Place_Stadium "TD Place Stadium").[[345]](#cite_note-346)[[346]](#cite_note-347) [Ottawa Rapid FC](/wiki/Ottawa_Rapid_FC "Ottawa Rapid FC") of the [Northern Super League](/wiki/Northern_Super_League "Northern Super League") also play at TD Place Stadium. The Rapid are one of the six founding clubs in the NSL, having played in the [inaugural 2025 season](/wiki/2025_Northern_Super_League_season "2025 Northern Super League season").[[347]](#cite_note-348) The [New York Riptide](/wiki/New_York_Riptide "New York Riptide") became [Ottawa Black Bears](/wiki/Ottawa_Black_Bears "Ottawa Black Bears") when they moved to the city in 2024.[[348]](#cite_note-349) They compete in the [National Lacrosse League](/wiki/National_Lacrosse_League "National Lacrosse League") and play at the [Canadian Tire Centre](/wiki/Canadian_Tire_Centre "Canadian Tire Centre") in [Kanata](/wiki/Kanata,_Ontario "Kanata, Ontario").[[349]](#cite_note-350) The [Ottawa Blackjacks](/wiki/Ottawa_Blackjacks "Ottawa Blackjacks") are a professional basketball team, playing in the [Canadian Elite Basketball League](/wiki/Canadian_Elite_Basketball_League "Canadian Elite Basketball League"), out of the TD Place Arena.[[350]](#cite_note-351)[[351]](#cite_note-352) The [Ottawa Titans](/wiki/Ottawa_Titans_(baseball) "Ottawa Titans (baseball)") play professional baseball in the [Frontier League](/wiki/Frontier_League "Frontier League") at [Raymond Chabot Grant Thornton Park](/wiki/Raymond_Chabot_Grant_Thornton_Park "Raymond Chabot Grant Thornton Park").[[352]](#cite_note-353) Ottawa was previously home to the [Ottawa Lynx](/wiki/Ottawa_Lynx "Ottawa Lynx"), a [Triple-A](/wiki/Triple-A_(baseball) "Triple-A (baseball)") club, as well as the [Ottawa Champions](/wiki/Ottawa_Champions "Ottawa Champions"), an independent baseball team in the [Can-Am League](/wiki/Can-Am_League "Can-Am League").[[353]](#cite_note-354)[[354]](#cite_note-355)

### Collegiate sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=35 "Edit section: Collegiate sports")]

The University of Ottawa and Carleton University [varsity teams](/wiki/Varsity_team "Varsity team") compete in [U Sports](/wiki/U_Sports "U Sports") in various sports.[[355]](#cite_note-356) Algonquin College and Collège La Cité teams compete in the [OCAA](/wiki/Ontario_Colleges_Athletic_Association "Ontario Colleges Athletic Association").[[356]](#cite_note-357)

The [Carleton Ravens](/wiki/Carleton_Ravens "Carleton Ravens") are nationally ranked in [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") and soccer as of 2011.[[357]](#cite_note-358) [Carleton's men's basketball](/wiki/Carleton_Ravens_men%27s_basketball "Carleton Ravens men's basketball") program is regarded as dynastically successful, having won 17 of the last 20 [national championships](/wiki/W._P._McGee_Trophy "W. P. McGee Trophy").[[358]](#cite_note-Carleton_Ravens_dynasty-359) The [Ottawa Gee-Gees](/wiki/Ottawa_Gee-Gees "Ottawa Gee-Gees") are nationally ranked in basketball and soccer as of 2022.[[359]](#cite_note-360)

### Non-professional and amateur sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=36 "Edit section: Non-professional and amateur sports")]

Several non-professional teams also play in Ottawa, including the [Ottawa 67's](/wiki/Ottawa_67%27s "Ottawa 67's") [junior ice hockey](/wiki/Junior_ice_hockey "Junior ice hockey") team.[[360]](#cite_note-KearneyRay2009-361)
The city is home to an assortment of amateur organized team sports such as [soccer](/wiki/Soccer "Soccer"), [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball"), [baseball](/wiki/Baseball "Baseball") and [curling](/wiki/Curling "Curling").[[361]](#cite_note-362) Casual recreational activities, such as [skating](/wiki/Ice_skating "Ice skating"), [cycling](/wiki/Cycling "Cycling") and [skiing](/wiki/Skiing "Skiing") are also available.[[362]](#cite_note-Sports-363)

The city has hosted [Ottawa Race Weekend](/wiki/Ottawa_Race_Weekend "Ottawa Race Weekend") during the 4th weekend of May since 1975. In addition, [Ironman Triathlon](/wiki/Ironman_Triathlon "Ironman Triathlon") began in Ottawa in 2025.[[363]](#cite_note-364)

## Government and politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=37 "Edit section: Government and politics")]

Further information: [List of Ottawa municipal elections](/wiki/List_of_Ottawa_municipal_elections "List of Ottawa municipal elections"), [Canadian federal election results in Ottawa](/wiki/Canadian_federal_election_results_in_Ottawa "Canadian federal election results in Ottawa"), and [List of embassies and high commissions in Ottawa](/wiki/List_of_embassies_and_high_commissions_in_Ottawa "List of embassies and high commissions in Ottawa")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/39/Ottawa_City_Hall_Hotel_de_ville_d%27Ottawa.jpg/250px-Ottawa_City_Hall_Hotel_de_ville_d%27Ottawa.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ottawa_City_Hall_Hotel_de_ville_d%27Ottawa.jpg)

[Ottawa City Hall](/wiki/Ottawa_City_Hall "Ottawa City Hall") houses the [seat of the local government](/wiki/Ottawa_City_Council "Ottawa City Council").

The [City of Ottawa](/wiki/City_of_Ottawa "City of Ottawa") is a [single-tier municipality](/wiki/Census_divisions_of_Ontario#Single-tier_municipalities "Census divisions of Ontario"), meaning it is in itself a [census division](/wiki/Census_division "Census division") and has no county or regional municipality government above it, and has no subsidiary municipalities to provide municipal services.[[364]](#cite_note-SlackChattopadhyay2009-365)[[365]](#cite_note-366)[[366]](#cite_note-367) Ottawa is governed by the 25-member [Ottawa City Council](/wiki/Ottawa_City_Council "Ottawa City Council") consisting of 24 councillors each representing one [ward](/wiki/Ward_(country_subdivision) "Ward (country subdivision)") and the mayor, [Mark Sutcliffe](/wiki/Mark_Sutcliffe "Mark Sutcliffe") as of the [2022 Ottawa municipal election](/wiki/2022_Ottawa_municipal_election "2022 Ottawa municipal election"), is elected in a citywide vote. Ottawa is subject to Ontario's [strong mayor powers](/wiki/Strong_Mayors,_Building_Homes_Act "Strong Mayors, Building Homes Act").[[367]](#cite_note-368)[[368]](#cite_note-369)

Along with being the capital of Canada, Ottawa is politically diverse in local politics. Most of the city has traditionally supported the [Liberal Party](/wiki/Liberal_Party_of_Canada "Liberal Party of Canada") in federal elections.[[369]](#cite_note-Hill2002-370)[[370]](#cite_note-371) The safest areas for the Liberals are the ones dominated by [Francophones](/wiki/Francophones "Francophones"), especially in Vanier and central Gloucester.[[369]](#cite_note-Hill2002-370) Central Ottawa is usually more [left](/wiki/Left-wing_politics "Left-wing politics")-leaning, and the [New Democratic Party](/wiki/New_Democratic_Party_(Canada) "New Democratic Party (Canada)") have won ridings there.[[371]](#cite_note-372) Ridings further outside the city centre, such as those including Kanata, Barrhaven and rural areas, tend to be more conservative, fiscally and socially.[[369]](#cite_note-Hill2002-370) This is especially true in the former Townships of [West Carleton](/wiki/West_Carleton_Township,_Ontario "West Carleton Township, Ontario"), [Goulbourn](/wiki/Goulbourn_Township,_Ontario "Goulbourn Township, Ontario"), [Rideau](/wiki/Rideau_Township,_Ontario "Rideau Township, Ontario") and [Osgoode](/wiki/Osgoode_Township,_Ontario "Osgoode Township, Ontario"), which are more in line with the conservative areas in the surrounding [counties](/wiki/County "County").[[369]](#cite_note-Hill2002-370)

At present, Ottawa is host to approximately 150 [embassies](/wiki/List_of_diplomatic_missions_in_Canada "List of diplomatic missions in Canada").[[372]](#cite_note-373) A further approximately 50 countries accredit their [embassies and missions in the United States](/wiki/List_of_diplomatic_missions_in_Canada#Accredited_Embassies_and_High_Commissions "List of diplomatic missions in Canada") to Canada.[[373]](#cite_note-374)

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=38 "Edit section: Transportation")]

See also: [OC Transpo](/wiki/OC_Transpo "OC Transpo"), [O-Train](/wiki/O-Train "O-Train"), [List of airports in the Ottawa area](/wiki/List_of_airports_in_the_Ottawa_area "List of airports in the Ottawa area"), [List of Ottawa roads](/wiki/List_of_Ottawa_roads "List of Ottawa roads"), [List of numbered roads in Ottawa](/wiki/List_of_numbered_roads_in_Ottawa "List of numbered roads in Ottawa"), and [List of bridges in Ottawa](/wiki/List_of_bridges_in_Ottawa "List of bridges in Ottawa")

### Public transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=39 "Edit section: Public transportation")]

Main article: [OC Transpo](/wiki/OC_Transpo "OC Transpo")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2a/O_Train_15773300606.jpg/250px-O_Train_15773300606.jpg)](/wiki/File:O_Train_15773300606.jpg)

An [O-Train](/wiki/O-Train "O-Train") crossing the [Rideau River](/wiki/Rideau_River "Rideau River"). The O-Train is a [light rail](/wiki/Light_rail "Light rail") [public transportation](/wiki/Public_transportation "Public transportation") service provided by [OC Transpo](/wiki/OC_Transpo "OC Transpo").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4e/Stage2South.png/250px-Stage2South.png)](/wiki/File:Stage2South.png)

Map of Ottawa's rapid transit network.

Ottawa's public transit system is managed by [OC Transpo](/wiki/OC_Transpo "OC Transpo").[[374]](#cite_note-oct-375) OC Transpo operates an integrated, multi-modal rapid transit system which includes:

* The [O-Train](/wiki/O-Train "O-Train") light rail system. The four-line public rail system includes three existing lines and one currently under construction.
  + [Line 1](/wiki/Line_1_(O-Train) "Line 1 (O-Train)") is an east–west line which operates medium-capacity light rail vehicles and travels under the city's downtown core.[[375]](#cite_note-376)
  + [Line 2](/wiki/Line_2_(O-Train) "Line 2 (O-Train)") is a north–south rail transit corridor which utilizes a mix of Stadler FLIRTs and Alstom Coradia LINTs connecting the south end of Ottawa to Line 1 at [Bayview station](/wiki/Bayview_station_(Ottawa) "Bayview station (Ottawa)").[[376]](#cite_note-377)[[377]](#cite_note-378)
  + [Line 3](/wiki/Line_3_(O-Train) "Line 3 (O-Train)") is an under construction branch of Line 1, splitting at [Lincoln Fields station](/wiki/Lincoln_Fields_station "Lincoln Fields station") and continuing west.[[378]](#cite_note-379)
  + [Line 4](/wiki/Line_4_(O-Train) "Line 4 (O-Train)") is a 4 km (2.5 mi) airport link connecting Line 2 to the [Ottawa Macdonald–Cartier International Airport](/wiki/Ottawa_Macdonald%E2%80%93Cartier_International_Airport "Ottawa Macdonald–Cartier International Airport")[[379]](#cite_note-380)
* A vast [bus rapid transit](/wiki/Bus_rapid_transit "Bus rapid transit") (BRT) system that uses a series of dedicated bus-only roadways named the [Transitway](/wiki/Transitway_(Ottawa) "Transitway (Ottawa)") and reserved lanes on city streets and highways. The Transitway has long distances between stops and full station amenities (including platforms, walkways, fare gates, ticket booths, elevators and convenience stores). It connects Ottawa's suburbs to the inner city.[[380]](#cite_note-381) The rapid bus service network operates all day, seven days a week, reaching the suburban communities of Kanata to the West, Barrhaven to the South-West, Orléans to the East, and South Keys to the South.[[381]](#cite_note-:1-382)
* Over 190 local bus routes are served by a fleet of ordinary, articulated and double-decker buses.[[381]](#cite_note-:1-382) Both OC Transpo and the Quebec-based [Société de transport de l'Outaouais](/wiki/Soci%C3%A9t%C3%A9_de_transport_de_l%27Outaouais "Société de transport de l'Outaouais") (STO) operate bus transit services between Ottawa and Gatineau.[[382]](#cite_note-383)[[383]](#cite_note-384) OC Transpo also operates a door-to-door bus service for disabled individuals known as ParaTranspo.[[374]](#cite_note-oct-375) There is a [hypothetical LRT partnership](/wiki/Gatineau_LRT "Gatineau LRT") that proposes linking Ottawa with Gatineau and connecting to the O-Train.[[384]](#cite_note-385)

### Airports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=40 "Edit section: Airports")]

The [Ottawa Macdonald–Cartier International Airport](/wiki/Ottawa_Macdonald%E2%80%93Cartier_International_Airport "Ottawa Macdonald–Cartier International Airport") is the city's principal airport.[[385]](#cite_note-386) There are also three other airports providing general aviation services: [Gatineau-Ottawa Executive Airport](/wiki/Gatineau-Ottawa_Executive_Airport "Gatineau-Ottawa Executive Airport"), [Ottawa/Carp Airport](/wiki/Carp_Airport "Carp Airport"), and [Ottawa/Rockcliffe Airport](/wiki/Ottawa/Rockcliffe_Airport "Ottawa/Rockcliffe Airport").[[386]](#cite_note-PurdomCarroll2003f-387)[[387]](#cite_note-388)[[388]](#cite_note-389)

### Inter-city transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=41 "Edit section: Inter-city transportation")]

[Ottawa station](/wiki/Ottawa_station "Ottawa station") is the [main](/wiki/Central_station "Central station") [inter-city](/wiki/Inter-city_rail "Inter-city rail") train station operated by [Via Rail](/wiki/Via_Rail "Via Rail"). It is located 4 kilometres (2.5 mi) to the east of [downtown](/wiki/Downtown_Ottawa "Downtown Ottawa") in [Eastway Gardens](/wiki/Eastway_Gardens "Eastway Gardens") (adjacent to [O-Train](/wiki/O-Train "O-Train") [Tremblay station](/wiki/Tremblay_station "Tremblay station")) and serves Via Rail's [Corridor](/wiki/Qu%C3%A9bec_City%E2%80%93Windsor_Corridor_(Via_Rail) "Québec City–Windsor Corridor (Via Rail)") Route.[[389]](#cite_note-390)[[390]](#cite_note-391)[[391]](#cite_note-392) The city is also served by inter-city passenger rail service at [Fallowfield station](/wiki/Fallowfield_station_(Ontario) "Fallowfield station (Ontario)") in the southwestern suburban community of [Barrhaven](/wiki/Barrhaven "Barrhaven").[[392]](#cite_note-393)

[Intercity bus services](/wiki/Intercity_bus_service "Intercity bus service") are currently provided by several carriers at various stops throughout the city, following the closure of the former [Ottawa Central Station](/wiki/Ottawa_Central_Station "Ottawa Central Station") [bus terminal](/wiki/Bus_station "Bus station") on 1 June 2021. Major carriers include: [Megabus](/wiki/Megabus_(North_America) "Megabus (North America)"), [Ontario Northland](/wiki/Ontario_Northland_Motor_Coach_Services "Ontario Northland Motor Coach Services"), Autobus Gatineau,[[393]](#cite_note-394)[[394]](#cite_note-395)[[395]](#cite_note-396) and [Orléans Express](/wiki/Orl%C3%A9ans_Express "Orléans Express").[[396]](#cite_note-397)

### Streets and highways

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=42 "Edit section: Streets and highways")]

The City of Ottawa has over 8,400 km (5,200 mi) lane-kilometres of road and a series of freeways.[[397]](#cite_note-398) The primary freeways are the east–west provincial [Highway 417](/wiki/Ontario_Highway_417 "Ontario Highway 417") (designated as the Queensway and part of the [Trans-Canada Highway](/wiki/Trans-Canada_Highway "Trans-Canada Highway")), Ottawa-Carleton [Highway 174](/wiki/Regional_Road_174 "Regional Road 174") (formerly Provincial Highway 17), [Highway 7](/wiki/Ontario_Highway_7 "Ontario Highway 7"), and the north–south provincial [Highway 416](/wiki/Ontario_Highway_416 "Ontario Highway 416") (designated as Veterans' Memorial Highway), which connects to other [400-Series highways](/wiki/400-series_highways_(Ontario) "400-series highways (Ontario)") via the 401.[[398]](#cite_note-399)[[399]](#cite_note-400)[[400]](#cite_note-:16-401) From downtown there are also freeway connections to [Autoroute 5](/wiki/Quebec_Autoroute_5 "Quebec Autoroute 5") and [Autoroute 50](/wiki/Quebec_Autoroute_50 "Quebec Autoroute 50"), in neighbouring Gatineau.[[401]](#cite_note-402)[[400]](#cite_note-:16-401)

The city also has several scenic parkways and promenades, such as the [Kichi Zibi Mikan](/wiki/Kichi_Zibi_Mikan "Kichi Zibi Mikan") (formerly the Macdonald Parkway), [Colonel By Drive](/wiki/Colonel_By_Drive "Colonel By Drive"), [Queen Elizabeth Driveway](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_Driveway "Queen Elizabeth Driveway"), the [Sir George-Étienne Cartier Parkway](/wiki/Sir_George-%C3%89tienne_Cartier_Parkway "Sir George-Étienne Cartier Parkway") (formerly the Rockcliffe Parkway), and the [Aviation Parkway](/wiki/Aviation_Parkway_(Ottawa) "Aviation Parkway (Ottawa)").[[402]](#cite_note-403) The National Capital Commission manages ceremonial routes linking key attractions on both sides of the Ottawa River, including [Confederation Boulevard](/wiki/Confederation_Boulevard "Confederation Boulevard").[[403]](#cite_note-404)

### Cycling and pedestrian network

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ottawa&action=edit&section=43 "Edit section: Cycling and pedestrian network")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/CapitalPathway2019.jpg/250px-CapitalPathway2019.jpg)](/wiki/File:CapitalPathway2019.jpg)

The [Capital Pathway](/wiki/Capital_Pathway "Capital Pathway") is a multi-use [trail](/wiki/Trail "Trail") interlinking sites throughout the [National Capital Region](/wiki/National_Capital_Region_(Canada) "National Capital Region (Canada)").

Numerous paved [multi-use trails](/wiki/Multi-use_trail "Multi-use trail"), mostly operated by the [National Capital Commission](/wiki/National_Capital_Commission "National Capital Commission") and the city, wind their way through much of the capital, including along the Ottawa River, Rideau River, and Rideau Canal.[[404]](#cite_note-405) [These pathways](/wiki/Capital_Pathway "Capital Pathway") are used for transportation, tourism, and recreation. Because many streets either have wide curb lanes or bicycle lanes, cycling is a mode of transportation used by up to 3.7% of citizens, including in winter. This is the largest percentage of any major Canadian city.[[405]](#cite_note-406)[[406]](#cite_note-407)[[407]](#cite_note-408)[[408]](#cite_note-409) As of 31 December 2015, over 900 km (560 mi) of cycling facilities are found in Ottawa, including 435 km (270 mi) of multi-use pathways, 8 km (5.0 mi) of cycle tracks, 200 km (120 mi) of on-road bicycle lanes, and 257 km (160 mi) of paved shoulders.[[409]](#cite_note-:0-410) 204 km (127 mi) of new cycling facilities were added between 2011 and 2014.[[409]](#cite_note-:0-410)

The entire length of [Sparks Street](/wiki/Sparks_Street "Sparks Street") was turned into a [pedestrian mall](/wiki/Pedestrian_zone "Pedestrian zone") in 1966.[[410]](#cite_note-411) Since 1960, additional avenues, streets, and parkways, are reserved for pedestrian and bicycle use only on Saturdays, Sundays and on selected holidays and events.[[411]](#cite_note-412) In 2021 city council unanimously approved the [Byward Market](/wiki/Byward_Market "Byward Market") Public Realm Plan to make the market area more [car-free](/wiki/Carfree_city "Carfree city") and pedestrian friendly.[[412]](#cite_note-Public_Realm_Plan-413) From 2009 to 2015 the NCC introduced the [Capital Bixi](/wiki/Capital_Bixi "Capital Bixi") [bicycle-sharing system](/wiki/Bicycle-sharing_system "Bicycle-sharing system"). This continued until the company VeloGo took over the program from 2015 to 2018 when the partnership ceased.[[413]](#cite_note-414)[[414]](#cite_note-415) [Scooter-sharing systems](/wiki/Scooter-sharing_system "Scooter-sharing system") were introduced in the downtown and inner-city areas in 2025.[[415]](#cite_note-416)
